title: Punniyarajan is revealed through previous birth
claim_or_observation: Aravanar and the Peedihai connect Punniyarajan with Āputhiran's previous life
notes: Rebirth language must remain within epic framework

title: Āputhiran reborn in Jāvagam
claim_or_observation: The text describes Āputhiran as reborn in Jāvagam to save lives of creatures
notes: Do not collapse previous and present identity

title: Punniyarajan raised by Bhumichandran
claim_or_observation: Childless king Bhumichandran raises the child who becomes king of Jāvagam

title: Punniyarajan learns abandonment history
claim_or_observation: He learns from Amarasundari that Sāli abandoned him as a baby in previous birth

title: Prosperity follows Punniyarajan's birth
claim_or_observation: The minister says drought ended and the earth flourished after the king's birth

title: Minister warns against abandoning subjects
claim_or_observation: Janamithran argues that a king's duty is protecting all lives and leaving would harm creatures

title: Punniyarajan chooses Manipallavam journey
claim_or_observation: The king says he cannot control his desire to visit Manipallavam and delegates protection temporarily

title: Peedihai reveals Aputhiran identity
claim_or_observation: The Dharma Peedihai shows Punniyarajan his previous birth as Āputhiran
notes: Memory as revelation not replacement identity

title: Punniyarajan remembers Amudhasurabhi service
claim_or_observation: He recalls receiving the vessel and feeding hungry people

title: Punniyarajan praises compassion of former life
claim_or_observation: He recognises former service removing hunger through Amudhasurabhi

title: Rebirth does not erase present person
claim_or_observation: Punniyarajan is not a simple continuation of Aputhiran but a king with new relationships and duties

title: King's spiritual crisis is governance test
claim_or_observation: His desire for liberation must be balanced against responsibility toward living subjects
